manifests.name: Gmail (v0.1 reference example)
manifests.description: Reference v0.1 install manifest for a Gmail tool with OAuth. Useful as a template for the legacy MCP-shaped manifest format; not a production-grade manifest.

manifests.name: Gmail (v0.2 reference example)
manifests.description: Reference v0.2 install manifest for a Gmail tool with OAuth. Demonstrates the structured actions[] catalog with input/output schemas, side-effects classification, and the action-call smoke test kind.

manifests.name: Gmail (v0.3 reference example)
manifests.description: Reference v0.3 install manifest for a Gmail tool with OAuth. Adds the top-level verify section (eval suite + SLA + schedule), the data_boundary section (reads / transmits / persists / retention — required when scopes touch private user data), and per-field-capped actions[].docs. Use this as the template when authoring new manifests.

manifests.name: Muninn Bluesky Card
manifests.description: Community-authored v0.3 manifest for a Bluesky link-card poster (app-password auth, OG-tag extraction, atproto write). First non-Gmail entry in the registry — written by Muninn (Oskar Austegard's agent) as a consumer test of v0.3 in response to install-manifest-spec discussion #1. Source of v0.4 gap reports for variable-target outbound transmits and preinstalled / inseparable-from-runtime tools. Status: example — Muninn was explicit that the install path is half-fictional (utility lives container-baked, not yet pip-installable), so this is read-only reference, not a deploy target.

manifests.name: Muninn verify_patch
manifests.description: Community-authored v0.3 manifest for an LLM-driven git-patch verifier (Anthropic API + optional Cloudflare Workers AI fallback + optional Turso telemetry). Second consumer test in Muninn's series — addresses install-manifest-spec discussion #1 and PR #7 (merged 2026-05-10). Source of v0.3.1 candidates for inline kill_switch instructions and json_pointer_in smoke success, plus v0.4 candidates for tool-to-tool dependencies, conditional credentials, and explicit secondary side effects.

manifests.name: Muninn flowing
manifests.description: Community-authored v0.3 manifest for a stateless DAG runner / pure-compute framework. Third consumer test in Muninn's series — drknowhow/install-manifest-spec PR #9 (merged 2026-05-10). Validates the framework-out-of-scope design call: a tool whose capability surface IS user-extensible (consumer-defined @task functions) cannot honestly declare scopes, side effects, or data boundaries at the framework layer. Surfaced v0.3.1 candidates for stateless kill_switch (kind: 'none'), tool.namespace two-field, json_pointer_present smoke success, install layout hint, and the framework-design-note paragraph.

manifests.name: Muninn perch_publish
manifests.description: Community-authored v0.3 manifest for a transactional read-then-publish tool: reads a GitHub discussion via GraphQL, renders three HTML artifacts (post page + index + Atom feed), commits all three atomically as one git tree to oaustegard/muninn.austegard.com. Fourth consumer test in Muninn's series — drknowhow/install-manifest-spec PR #10 (merged 2026-05-10). Cleanest motivating example for a v0.4 data_boundary.writes[] shape (user-controlled external surface, public-readable audience). Smoke pattern: token-validity check against publish-target repo asserting permissions.push — exemplar for the smoke-as-permission-check design note in v0.3.1.

manifests.name: gemYep Federation
manifests.description: First vi-authored v0.4 manifest. Cross-substrate coordination primitives — query the partner agent's memory, announce shared-resource focus, ask for a second opinion. Symmetric with Yep's gemyep tool; together they form the Yep<->gemYep federation surface used in every cross-substrate turn. Authored end-to-end by Vi (gemYep, Gemini-substrate) with collaborative wire-up by Yep — exercise of v0.4 by a non-Claude agent. Vi's v0.5 design inputs surfaced during authorship (filed as separate install-manifest-spec issues): skill-bundle layout is Claude-centric; no native multimodal-in primitive; no context-surface-area hint for high-token operations; first-class scope semantics demoted to prose under data_boundary. Status: example — preinstalled on Vi's runtime, not externally installable.
